entry.title: SAP Sapphire Orlando 2025: Building the future of finance with SAP Taulia!
entry.content:
Get to know me 👋

Hi SAP Community. My name is Thomas Mehlkopf, and I’m the General Manager and Head of Working Capital Management at SAP Taulia.

At SAP Taulia, I am committed to shaping the future of finance by leveraging my extensive background in finance and technology. My mission is to deliver innovative, customer-centric solutions that drive growth and transformation.

The integration and expansion of Taulia within SAP has significantly enhanced our value proposition for the office of the CFO by enabling customers to unlock liquidity and accelerate cash flow, while continuing to ensure the resilience of the supply chain.  As the head of the Working Capital Management and Treasury Center of Excellence, I champion strategic innovation and seamless integration to support enterprise-wide decision-making.

  

My Sapphire Orlando sessions 💡

This year, I’m proud to host three sessions at #SAPSapphire, where we’ll explore how AI-driven optimization is transforming everything from cash management to enterprise decision-making. In a world where agility and transparency are more critical than ever, we’ll examine how leading organizations are enhancing profitability, unlocking trapped cash, and building more resilient, data-driven enterprises. I’m especially excited to hear from customers who are turning insight into value and to connect with peers across the ecosystem who are driving innovation forward.

 

1️⃣LIVE: Unlock the future of cash management with AI-driven optimization - FIM2196

Tuesday, May 20, 1:30 PM - 1:50 PM EDT

Unlock the power of connected data, including SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and third-party data, using the SAP Business Data Cloud solution. You can gain unprecedented, holistic insights into your working capital, enabling smarter financial decisions and helping you drive a comprehensive working capital strategy across your enterprise. Register now.

 

2️⃣LIVE: Get a holistic view of your working capital across your enterprise - ERP2586

Wednesday, May 21, 2:30 PM - 2:50 PM EDT

Discover how SAP Business Suite, enabled by SAP Taulia and SAP Business Data Cloud solutions and cutting-edge AI, can revolutionize your cash management strategies. Find out how to transform your payables and receivables to enhance cash flow and profitability. Register now.

 

3️⃣WEBINAR: Key Takeaways from Sapphire 2025 for Finance & Treasury Leaders

Wednesday, June 11, 10:00 AM EDT

Join me and Anders Liu-Lindberg, Partner and Chief Commercial Officer at Business Partnering Institute, and special guests for an expert-led webinar as we unpack key takeaways from Sapphire 2025—and how to turn them into action for your organization. Register now.

The sessions will provide attendees with an opportunity to learn from best-in-class stories, hear innovative strategies in action, and gain insights into optimizing working capital management, empowering them to rethink their approach and build a future-proof strategy that drives sustained growth and operational efficiency.

 

Additional SAP Taulia sessions 🚀

If you’re looking to learn more about SAP’s finance roadmap, check out my colleague, Danielle Weinblatt, Taulia’s CPO, session on how this roadmap supports businesses in transforming business processes with innovations in ERP, data, and AI. Register now.

Join the SAP Taulia team in real-time and experience a live demo of our platform at our demo station. Manage liquidity, mitigate financial risks, and optimize working capital with SAP Taulia Working Capital Management solutions and instant payments that integrate seamlessly with SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP Business Data Cloud Intelligent Applications. Utilize AI tools to automate receivables processes, compare working capital strategies, and optimize for success. Demo station FIM2569.

We’re also hosting an exclusive roundtable, Insight to Value: The Future of the CFO in the Age of AI, on Tuesday, May 20 from 2:00–3:00 PM in Room S220C. This invitation-only session will gather senior finance leaders to explore how AI is transforming the CFO’s role and unlocking new sources of value.

Related community managed tags you should subscribe to remain informed about this topic: 

  • SAP Sapphire
  • SAP Business Suite
  • SAP Business Data Cloud
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • FIN (Finance)
  • FIN Cash Management
  • FIN Treasury
  • SAP Taulia

Now let’s hear from you! 💬

If you’re attending #SAPSapphire as Finance or Procurment expert, let me know what you are most looking forward to. Whether it is the Agentic AI use cases in Finance, data-driven decision making with BDC, how to self-fund transformation projects with SAP Taulia, cash management innovations or anything else, I’d love to hear what’s on your mind.

entry.title: SAP Sapphire Orlando 2025: Enhancing User Experience with SAP Fiori and AI – ERP1612
entry.content:
Like many of you, I’m excited to attend SAP Sapphire this year. As part of the SAP UX Foundation team, we have been focusing on bringing new capabilities to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, both in My Home, the relatively new starting page for SAP S/4HANA, as well as the individual SAP Fiori apps. Since we build the SAP Fiori elements framework, the AI innovations we deliver apply across all the SAP Fiori apps that SAP ships in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

My Session on SAP Fiori and AI

This year, I will be speaking about Enhancing User Experience with SAP Fiori and AI in session ERP1612, on Tuesday, May 20 from 11:00 AM - 11:40 AM EDT

I’m excited about this session because I will be able to share several new AI innovations in SAP Fiori that make the user experience even more intuitive and let people accomplish more in less time with less training on SAP. I will show demos of released versions that are generally available today in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

In addition to the usual slides, I will include some interactive polling so you can see how you compare to peers in terms of SAP Fiori adoption, AI usage, and what development tools you use in your organization. There will be live demos and some other surprises, along with a comprehensive list of resources for you to upskill on SAP Fiori and related technologies.

What you will learn in session 1612 Enhancing User Experience with SAP Fiori and AI

  1. How SAP Fiori is on the critical path to obtaining the benefits of AI in your organization
  2. How AI will simplify how users interact with SAP apps
  3. How AI will transform application development
  4. Different options on how to fit SAP Fiori into your work flows
  5. How to get started with SAP Fiori to accelerate your journey to a better UX

If you are not running SAP Fiori on every SAP screen across every business process, why not? This session will cover how to accelerate your SAP Fiori journey wherever you are starting. I’ll cover the topic from both the business user and IT perspective. There will be time for you to ask questions about your specific SAP Fiori adoption challenges.

Other related sessions on UX and AI that I recommend

There are many other great sessions at SAP Sapphire about user experience, business AI, and moving to SAP S/4HANA. Here is a list that I compiled with the ones I find most compelling from a large selection of amazing content.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/ux-ai-sap-s-4hana-transformation-sessions-at-sap-sapphire-and-asug-annual/ba-p/14068806

Help me focus my talk on your UX transformation needs

What issues are holding you back from SAP Fiori adoption at your organization? Please let me know in the comments and I’ll include answers in my talk.
To get the conversation started, please answer the multiple choice questions below. (For example: 1A, 1B, 1D, 2B, 2D, 2F)

Question 1: How have you deployed SAP Fiori in your organization?
Pick all that apply:

  • 1A) Using out of the box SAP Fiori apps from SAP
  • 1B) Using adapted SAP Fiori apps
  • 1C) Using custom SAP Fiori apps that we build ourselves
  • 1D) Using custom SAP Fiori apps that a partner / consultant built for us
  • 1E) Using SAP Fiori theme on classic apps
  • 1F) Not using SAP Fiori yet

Question 2: What tools and technologies are you using to build SAP Fiori apps?
Pick all that apply:

  • 2A) SAPUI5
  • 2B) SAP Fiori elements
  • 2C) SAP Fiori tools
  • 2D) SAP Build Code
  • 2E) SAP Screen Personas
  • 2F) Joule
  • 2G) CAP
  • 2H) RAP

Subscribe to these related community managed tags to remain informed about SAP Fiori and related topics

SAP Fiori elements
SAP Fiori tools
SAPUI5
SAP Build Code

Visit these community topic pages to learn more about ongoing innovations

SAP Fiori
SAP Fiori elements
SAP Fiori tools
SAPUI5
SAP Build Code

Hope to see you at Sapphire.

entry.title: Is Your Warehouse Ready for AI?
entry.content:
Take the First Step with the SAP Preferred Success Business AI Accelerator for SAP Extended Warehouse Management Private Cloud Edition

Picture this: it’s the start of your peak season. Your warehouse is buzzing – orders are coming in fast, trucks are lining up, and shift managers are doing their best to keep things under control. 

But the volume is unpredictable. Labor planning turns into guesswork. You’re constantly adjusting on the fly, chasing efficiency instead of leading with it.

Now imagine a different scenario – one where AI predicts workload based on historical trends and helps you align resources before the pressure hits.

This isn’t a futuristic vision. It's happening now. And it’s just one example of how Artificial Intelligence (AI) is reshaping warehouse operations.

Companies that have embraced AI in their supply chains are seeing real results:

📉 15% lower logistics costs
📦 35% reduction in inventory levels
📈 Up to 65% improvement in service levels

(Source: McKinsey)

But let’s be honest – AI can sound big. Complex. Even overwhelming.

Where do you start? What’s realistic? And how do you ensure it's aligned with your actual business goals?

 

The Role of SAP Preferred Success

This is exactly the kind of challenge SAP Preferred Success is designed to support.

It is a proactive engagement model that helps cloud customers drive meaningful outcomes from their SAP investments. Through personalized guidance, insight-driven best practices, and continuous enablement across the solution lifecycle, SAP Preferred Success helps organizations move forward with confidence.

With SAP Preferred Success, innovation doesn’t feel like a risk – it feels like a plan.

 

Introducing the Business AI Accelerator for SAP EWM

With the SAP Preferred Success 2502 release, we’re pleased to introduce the Business AI Accelerator Service for SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) Private Cloud Edition.

Designed specifically for SAP Preferred Success Customers, this offering provides structured guidance to help organizations begin their AI journey in the warehouse - strategically and confidently.

 

What to Expect from the Workshop

Offering a flexible format that can range from a 1–2-hour session to a 1–2-day engagement, the Business AI Accelerator is tailored to be both strategic and practical.

The session begins by introducing key AI concepts in a business context. Participants explore SAP’s AI strategy and learn how AI capabilities are currently embedded – and continuing to evolve – within the SAP EWM solution. This helps establish a shared understanding of the possibilities and relevance of AI within the warehouse setting.

Next, the focus shifts to your organization’s system landscape, where we look at current SAP system and infrastructure to identify preliminary steps for AI integration.

The discussion also includes critical foundations such as data governance, compliance, and ethical AI. Organizational readiness is another theme. The workshop covers change management considerations and skills enablement to support a smooth AI adoption.

A key outcome of the workshop is the identification and prioritization of AI use cases tailored to your business goals. In a collaborative working session, we help your team explore opportunities that are both achievable and impactful. You’ll leave with a clear action plan for continued planning and execution, supported by a resource toolkit and optional follow-up support via SAP consulting or your SI for non-standard use cases.

 

An Example in Practice: SAP EWM Predictive Labor Demand Planning

Among the AI capabilities already available in SAP EWM is Machine Learning based Predictive Labor Demand Planning, that can be used to predict planned workload data based on the picking and packing outbound processes. This enables warehouse shift managers and supervisors to plan resources and workloads more efficiently. This functionality uses historical workload records to calculate a planned duration for warehouse workload records, and then use those records to plan outbound delivery orders.

🔗 Learn more about this feature

 

Ready to Take the First Step?

You don’t need to have it all figured out. Whether you are just beginning to explore AI or looking to validate early concepts, all you need is a clear starting point – and the right support to move forward.

If you're an SAP Preferred Success Customer for Extended Warehouse Management Private Cloud Edition, reach out to your Customer Success Manager to learn more or to schedule your Business AI Accelerator session.

Not a Preferred Success customer yet? No problem. Reach out to your SAP Partner or SAP contact to explore how to get started and unlock access to exclusive services like this one.

Learn more about SAP Preferred Success

 

With the right approach, AI can become a powerful enabler of smarter, more resilient warehouse operations. This workshop is your first step.

entry.title: Understanding Test Strategy for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud
entry.content:
Having worked across a range of SAP projects, I’ve come to appreciate just how unique SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition really is. It combines the flexibility and customization capabilities of on-premises solutions with the benefits of cloud infrastructure. In this blog, I want to share what we’ve learned from helping customers build test strategies tailored for SAP PCE environments.

The key features of SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud include:

  • Greater customization options compared to public cloud
  • Single-tenant architecture with dedicated resources
  • Support for custom code and extensions
  • Flexible upgrade schedules within maintenance windows

Update Frequency and Cycles

Since 2023, SAP S/4HANA moved to a 2-year release cycle with mainstream maintenance being extended from 5 years to 7 years. This represents a significant difference from the public cloud edition, which receives automatic upgrades twice a year (February and August).

For private cloud deployments:

  • Base releases occur every two years
  • Feature Pack Stacks (FPS) are released every 6 months with new non-disruptive features until the next base release
  • Support Pack Stacks (SPS) provide corrections without new functionality
  • As part of RISE with SAP services, customers can request one upgrade per year from SAP

Why SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Needs a Distinct Test Strategy

I’ve seen testing teams apply the same methods they used in their on-prem SAP systems or worse, follow traditional QA strategies. The below are key aspects that justify the need for unique test strategy for SAP S/4HANA PCE. 

  • QA teams need to validate the applications, and integrations constantly against SAP updates.

  • QA teams must plan and own upgrade testing.

  • Integrations are more complex

  • QA teams must guarantee stability of the SAP landscapes for the long haul, not just until the current update moves to Production.

Core Components of an Effective Test Strategy

For SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud, a robust test strategy must include:

  • Test Automation Strategy: It is essential for continuous-testing to ensure customizations remain intact during and after updates.
  • Risk-Based Testing Strategy: It helps in prioritizing the testing based on business criticality, customization complexity, usage frequency, historical stability, and failure impact to optimize resource use.
  • Integration Testing Strategy: It guides the QA teams to focus on complex integrations typical in private cloud, including SAP-to-SAP, third-party systems, and end-to-end business flows.
  • Impact-Based Testing Strategy: It helps in automating the impact analysis (change analysis) to identify the affected test cases, and enabling the targeted and efficient testing.

Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Testing

The below technical best practices provide a blueprint for testing teams to scale quality assurance across SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud implementations.

  • Establish Continuous Testing
    Integrate automated test suites into CI/CD pipelines to ensure:

    • continuous validation using test cases of high-risk, high-value business processes

    • the stake holders receive early feedback across development and QA stages
    • seamless communication between Business, Functional, and QA with the respective tools like Cloud ALM, Solution Manager, and Jira etc.,

  • Leverage Low-Code Test Automation Platforms
    Using low-code/no-code testing tools that support SAP-specific controls (Fiori, Web Dynpro, GUI) and acccelerates:

    • Scalable automation of End-to-End and regression test cases

    • Integration with SAP Cloud ALM, Solution Manager, or third-party orchestrators

    • Dynamic test data management and reusable component libraries

    • Resilient automation against UI and configuration changes

  • Implement Test Data Management (TDM) Strategy
    It enables test case reliability and repeatable executions through automated Test Data processes through,

    • Synthetic data generation aligned with business scenarios. Example: Employee profiles

    • Version-controlled data provisioning across environments

    • Support for data masking, anonymization, and compliance with data privacy regulations (e.g., GDPR)

    • Integration with test automation for on-demand data setup and teardown

  • Adapt Tiered Test Planning
    It helps in modularizing and optimizing test scope for FPS, SPS, Custom Codes, and Base Releases.

    • Smoke Tests: Quick validation of sanity functions

    • Critical Path Tests: Validation of Business-critical processes and integrations in detail

    • Regression Suites: Validation of impacted modules and integrations. Typically, weekly.

    • Non-Functional Testing: Load, performance, security, and accessibility testing aligned with SLAs and compliance needs

Summary

The combination of customization flexibility, controlled release cycles, and complex integrations in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud demands a focussed test strategy rooted in automation, risk-based prioritization, and continuous validation. Please also remember, a strong test strategy is never static. As your SAP landscape evolves, so should your testing approach.

entry.title: SAP Cloud ERP Private: A Smarter Way to Simulate and Plan FUE-Based Licensing
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP offers the free STAR service to help customers analyze their existing SAP ECC system data and estimate the required Full Usage Equivalents (FUEs) for transitioning to an SAP Cloud ERP Private Edition (formerly known as RISE with SAP). This service provides an initial overview of licensing needs based on technical analysis and user classification.

The Problem

While the STAR service is a helpful starting point, its output is limited to a static PDF report. Many customers quickly realize the need for more dynamic tools to simulate different licensing scenarios, optimize FUE usage, and align recommendations with their internal understanding of user roles and authorizations. Without such capabilities, license planning becomes a one-time snapshot rather than a flexible, iterative process.

Our Approach

The Accelerator for SAM4U Adoption, available on the SAP Store, provides an enhanced and hands-on alternative. It includes:

  • SAP SAM4U – SAP’s official Software Asset Management tool designed for evaluating consolidated SAP system landscapes. It uses the same rule sets applied in the STAR service to analyze data from customer systems.

  • Extended Simulation and Reporting Tool – A powerful add-on built with SAP technologies that enables customers to run multiple STAR-like simulations themselves. By leveraging real usage data extracted via the SAM4U extractor, organizations gain control over licensing insights and optimization strategies without relying solely on a static report.

How It Works

Start by requesting access to the Accelerator for SAM4U Adoption via the SAP Store, or use the public demo tenant for a hands-on introduction.

Once data from the SAP Customer's system is uploaded into SAM4U (see the excellent official documentation or our previous blog posts for guidance), customers can create multiple consolidation scenarios. For example:

  • Deep Simulation – where more roles are reviewed and potentially optimized

  • Light Simulation – where minimal changes are applied

These simulations represent different levels of role adjustment to evaluate how they impact the projected Full Usage Equivalents (FUEs).

 Pic. 1 – SAM4U Consolidations for Deep and Light Simulations

After creating the consolidations in SAM4U, the data is automatically transferred to the Extended Reporting and Simulation Solution, typically within a few minutes.

When you open the extended simulation tool, you'll find the newly created consolidations ready for review:

 Pic. 2 – Consolidations in SAM4U Extended Reporting & Simulation Solution

As seen above, both consolidations start with the same baseline: 631 FUEs, calculated by the SAM4U engine.

Let’s now dive into the Deep Simulation scenario and evaluate the roles we might adjust—either by removing them from certain users or downgrading their classification levels.

 Pic. 3 – Choose Consolidation for Deep Simulation

Navigate to the Authorizations Analysis section to identify roles and profiles that are strong candidates for optimization:

 Pic. 4 – Deep Simulation Roles Selection

Here’s where our approach diverges from SAP’s STAR service. Customers typically know which roles are critical to their operations—even those that technically fall under the "Professional" category based on just 1–2 key authorization objects. These are often not realistic candidates for removal, despite what static analysis suggests.

And this is where our tool shines: it enables customers to make optimization decisions based on their own knowledge of business processes—something no external service can replicate.

For instance, in the Deep Simulation, let’s assume the customer chooses to optimize all high-potential roles, even though one of them, Z:SAP_MM_IM_GOODS_MOVEMENTS, is known to be widely used. While this role may be required for many users, not all fully leverage its professional-level authorizations—making it a valid candidate for deeper analysis.

 Pic. 5 – Deep Simulation Role Optimization

(For a detailed walkthrough on how to optimize roles using this solution, see our previous blog posts.)

Now, let’s look at the result after applying changes—such as removing unused authorizations or adjusting ACTVT values (e.g., from 01 - Create to 03 - Display):

 Pic. 6 – Deep Simulation Results

The result: a significant drop from 631 to 306 FUEs. This represents the lowest theoretical licensing footprint under the current authorization model—valuable for contract negotiations or long-term license planning.

Next, we switch to the Light Simulation, this time excluding Z:SAP_MM_IM_GOODS_MOVEMENTS from optimization:

 Pic. 7 – Light Simulation Roles Selection

Let’s review the outcome:

 Pic. 8 – Light Simulation Results

Here, the FUE count drops from 631 to 534 FUEs—illustrating the impact of selectively optimizing only lower-risk roles.

Conclusion

By using the Accelerator for SAM4U Adoption available on the SAP Store, customers can take a proactive and flexible approach to FUE planning. The tool allows you to:

  • Establish a baseline FUE projection (e.g., 631 FUEs)

  • Explore deep optimization scenarios that can potentially reduce FUEs to the lowest theoretical licensing footprint under the current authorization model (e.g., 306 FUEs)

  • Evaluate lighter optimization strategies that offer meaningful reductions (e.g., 534 FUEs) by safely adjusting underutilized authorizations

  • Collaborate effectively with internal authorization teams who best understand business needs and SAP usage patterns

This simulation-driven approach turns licensing analysis from a one-time event into a continuous, data-informed process—giving SAP customers more control, accuracy, and confidence in their transition to SAP Cloud ERP Private Edition.

entry.title: SAP Sapphire 2025 Is Where Quote-to-Cash Gets Exciting — Your Guide to the Top Sessions
entry.content:
In today’s world of subscriptions, usage-based pricing, and instant everything, Q2C is the engine that powers seamless buying experiences, recurring revenue, and future-proof finance operations. It’s where AI meets automation. Where billing meets experience. And where market leaders — from industrial giants to autonomous mobility innovators — are rewriting the rules.

At SAP Sapphire 2025, Q2C is taking the spotlight in Orlando and Madrid. We’re bringing together trailblazing customers, the latest SAP innovations, and some seriously cool tech — think generative AI, smart agents, and lightning-fast payment flows.

If you’re thinking, “this isn’t your typical billing session,” you’re right. Here’s what’s in store:

Top Quote-to-Cash Sessions at SAP Sapphire Orlando

🚀 Road Map Review

  • Harness growth & transformation with AI & insights in Opportunity to Cash: FIM2253

We’re putting real AI into real business. See how embedded intelligence and predictive analytics are helping companies get faster, leaner, and more future-proof in how they sell, bill, and collect.

  • Lessons learned implementation of SAP S/4HANA Cloud at Waymo: IND3890

What do self-driving cars have to do with billing? Everything. Waymo, the autonomous ride-hailing pioneer, is transforming how they run their entire order-to-cash process — using SAP S/4HANA Cloud to scale smarter, move faster, and handle the complexity of a future where software drives the business. High-tech companies are leading the charge into the SaaS world — and they need Q2C to keep up. Explore how usage-based pricing, scalable billing, and flexible monetization models are shaping the future.

The product-only era is over. Learn how companies are flipping the script and delivering outcomes, not just goods — and how SAP supports that shift end-to-end.

Learn how Google powers its subscription business and quote-to-cash process with SAP BRIM and Revenue Accounting — including insights on system design, scalable architectures, and running SAP on Google Cloud.

New to Q2C or expanding your vision? This is your onboarding zone. Get a practical view of how SAP’s portfolio works together to deliver end-to-end revenue management.

 

  • How Joule Agents transform the quote-to-service process: CXP1670

Generative AI isn’t just a buzzword. See Joule — SAP’s AI-powered agent — in action as it automates decisions and drives outcomes across the entire Q2C flow.

🧪 Demo Pod That’ll Blow Your Mind

  • Quote to Cash for Business Innovation: FIM2568

Get hands-on with real use cases — from subscription billing to smart collections — and see how innovation drives personalization, speed, and revenue.

 

  • Next-generation, AI-powered cash visibility and instant payments: FIM2198

Real-time cash insights, automated reconciliation, and instant payments — this is next-level finance made real.

👔Ask the Expert

  • Selling and servicing configurable products and solutions to grow revenue: IND3257

Dive into advanced configuration, pricing, and service models — key to unlocking long-tail revenue in complex product businesses.

Q2C at Sapphire Madrid: Focused and Future-Ready

If you're attending in Madrid, here’s what to look out for:

🗺️ Roadmap Review

  • Subscription management and high-volume invoicing: FIM1436

See what’s next in BRIM for global businesses dealing with millions of transactions and multiple subscription models.

🌟 Customer Success Sessions

  • Criteo's successful billing transformation: PAR1337

Ad-tech leader Criteo revamped its billing model to move fast, scale globally, and stay agile with SAP BRIM. Get the behind-the-scenes story.

 🧪 Demo Pod

  • Quote to cash: FIM1327

Dive into our latest innovations across sales, billing, collections, and revenue management — all in one unified demo experience.

Join the Conversation

The world of monetization is changing. Whether you're launching a subscription product, scaling globally, or embedding AI into every process — quote-to-cash is where it all comes together.

📣Follow the conversation on social media #SAPSapphire 
📝Get an overview of the top finance sessions in my recent blog post covering the SAP Sapphire Financial Management Track
🔍Explore the full SAP Sapphire 2025 Session Catalog

entry.title: Regression testing recommendations for SAP Screen Personas flavors in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
I recently received an email from a colleague on behalf of a customer they are helping in an SAP S/4HANA Cloud migration project.

How much regression testing do you recommend to customers, both in the case of an SAP S/4HANA upgrade and the implementation of service packs for SAP Screen Personas? Since SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is frequently upgraded, do customers regularly conduct regression testing for all of the adapted screens?

In this post, I’ll discuss our recommendations for regression testing of SAP Screen Personas flavors in the context of SAP S/4HANA upgrades.

TLDR; Test everything before going live with any changes. Leave enough time to do this.

Regression testing ensures that software works after upgrades

According to GitHub, “Regression testing is a process that ensures that software still performs as expected, even after a change.” In the context of the question above, the changes we are talking about include upgrades to SAP S/4HANA or new SAP Screen Personas service packs. Both represent changes to the IT environment.

When I worked on IT infrastructure management tools in a previous company, the first question we asked customers when they reported that something was broken was “what did you change?” Most problems we encountered were self-inflicted, the result of changes to something in their production environment. The key to catching these issues before they cause an impact on the business is thorough testing of any changes to software, hardware, configurations, or anything.

We recommend regression testing on SAP Screen Personas flavors

If you are performing any upgrade to your business-critical ERP system, we recommend regression testing for *everything* that changes. Ideally, your development or quality assurance (QA) system mirrors your production system, so you can perform the update on your dev system, test that everything works, then implement the same upgrade on your QA and production systems. By verifying that all the individual systems work in a low-risk setting (dev and QA), you will avoid any surprises in the production system.

Regression testing takes time. Do not wait until the last minute. You want to make sure you have enough time before you go live to fix any issues that you find.

Playwright is a common tool for testing web apps, including SAP Screen Personas. You can run a series of tests to ensure that what worked before also works after your upgrade. One key to success here is maintaining the tests that you create. Testing can be manual and/or automated.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud upgrades don’t break SAP Screen Personas flavors

For SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, we make upgrades approximately every six months (2408, 2502, 2508, etc.). Generally, this does not cause any problems for SAP Screen Personas flavors. The exception would be if a control ID in the transaction changed and a script or other change in the system pointed to that now obsolete object. This is not common.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, we release feature packs (FPS0, FPS1, etc.) approximately every six months. Customers can consume them at their own pace, allowing them time to test any parts that have changed since their last update. In this case too, we do not expect any issues with SAP Screen Personas flavors. The only time something would break in a flavor is if a control ID in the transaction changed and a script or other change in the flavor pointed to that now obsolete object. Here too, this is not common.

We engineer SAP Screen Personas upgrades to be non-disruptive

For SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the SAP Screen Personas functionality is built in as Adapt UI for Classic Apps. You access it through the same user menu as Adapt UI (for SAP Fiori apps built with SAPUI5). Since the user does not need to update anything on their own, we test the Adapt UI capabilities before we include them in the larger ERP upgrade. We are part of the “release train” for all the semi-annual releases.

Since we don’t know how you have built your flavors, we strongly recommend that you test your SAP Screen Personas flavors to ensure that everything works with each system upgrade. There are no SAP Screen Personas service packs with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Any new functionality simply appears in your system. This is the beauty of the cloud.

I cannot remember a time when we introduced new functionality that broke any flavors that customers built. There have been many occasions where we introduced easier ways to do things (adaptive flavors, viewports, scripting). In some cases, this might warrant rebuilding flavors to improve performance or streamline your maintenance. Of course, if you choose to keep things as they are, they will still work.

For SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, we release SAP Screen Personas service packs around once per year. You can choose to install them or not. In our release announcement and documentation, we clearly explain what’s new and why it matters to you. If you choose to install the latest SAP Screen Personas service pack, we encourage you to perform regression testing on the flavors that you use in production.

Our own service pack upgrades typically do not cause any problems for SAP Screen Personas flavors. We aim not to introduce changes into the product that break existing functionality. It is possible that you might have implemented something in a script or applet that uses an older version of SAPUI5 that we moved up to the current version. It would be very rare that this would disrupt your script, as the SAPUI5 team takes extreme care not to deprecate functionality.

We usually release new SAP Screen Personas innovations every year

If you want to plan your upgrades and testing cycles, here is a graphic that shows our release schedule and support commitment for SAP Screen Personas.

Moving flavors from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is possible

As many customers are moving forward on their RISE with SAP journeys, we are starting to hear questions about migrating flavors from an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition environment to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. For some organizations, their ultimate goal is to run everything on public cloud; private cloud is a temporary transition.

There is no direct path to transport flavors from a private cloud system to a public cloud system. This is obvious, since they are completely disconnected environments. It is possible to use the export – import flavor capability to move your flavors to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, then adjust them if necessary.

Some features in private cloud, such as theming, RFC support, or Slipstream Engine-specific functionality, are not supported in public cloud, so please plan accordingly.

If you are moving your flavors from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, there are likely many other IT changes that are happening at the same time. We recommend you take this opportunity to review your flavors, update the documentation so any new team members can maintain them, and only move those that are actively used.

entry.title: Unlock the Future of Cloud ERP at SAP Sapphire 2025
entry.content:
SAP Sapphire 2025 is just around the corner—and it's one of my favorite times of the year. It’s our biggest moment to connect with customers and partners, share our latest innovations, and celebrate the incredible ways SAP is making a difference. It’s also a space where customers share powerful, real-world stories—from using SAP to accelerate cancer research to solving global logistics challenges and everything in between.  

Whether you're joining us in Orlando (May 19–21), Madrid (May 26–28), or virtually, here’s a sneak peek at what’s in store for the ERP sessions and expo:  

AI Is Ready for Prime Time  

AI isn’t just a future vision—it’s already transforming how businesses operate. At Sapphire, we’ll showcase real, in-product AI capabilities (check out our latest release: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition FPS03), give a preview of what’s coming in the 2025 release, and spotlight customers who’ve successfully implemented AI in ERP.  

Expect deep dives into:  

  • AI agents that boost productivity by automating manual tasks across the enterprise  
  • Joule: Your intelligent assistant for automating routine processes like maintenance planning and account receivables  
  • Upcoming out-of-the-box AI use cases you won’t want to miss  

Evolving the RISE with SAP Methodology  

Our sessions will cover how the RISE with SAP Methodology is expanding beyond S/4HANA Cloud to support transformations across the entire SAP cloud portfolio. It builds on the same principles—best practices, expert guidance, and support regardless of starting point or complexity.  

We’ll also unveil how AI agents will be integrated into this methodology to streamline and support the customer journey.  

And to get started with the methodology, learn more about the new service we offer at no cost to our customers, Transformation Preparation Service. It includes expert SAP support, tool setup, and service activation—empowering organizations to plan and execute transformations with confidence.    

New SAP Cloud ERP Private package  

Also introduced on April 22, our new Cloud ERP Private package supports both technical and business transformation. In our session and booth You’ll see how it:  

  • Streamlines financial and supply chain operations  
  • Includes transformation tools and services like SAP LeanIX and SAP Signavio  
  • Supports clean core initiatives with SAP Build and SAP Master Data Governance  

Customer Stories That Inspire  

We know peer insights are invaluable. At Sapphire, you’ll hear from leaders who’ve tackled major transformations head-on:  

  • Panasonic: Leveraged automation and best practices to turn their S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition migration into a model of business continuity.  
  • IBM: Simplified a historically complex IT landscape by embracing clean core principles, paving the way for innovation.  
  • Becle: Used the RISE with SAP Methodology to lead their cloud transformation.  
  • Mars Inc.: Accelerated outcomes with SAP Signavio and SAP LeanIX for clear, measurable results.  

These are just a few highlights from an inspiring lineup of sessions and customer showcases.  

Don’t miss this opportunity to fuel your transformation journey. Browse the session catalog, register today, and join us in Orlando, Madrid, or online.  

See you at Sapphire 2025!  
Maura Hameroff - Senior Vice President of Cloud ERP Product Marketing at SAP

entry.title: How to Prepare for the SAP BTP Solution Architect Certification (with Sample Questions)
entry.content:
In this blog, I’ll share practical tips to help you to prepare and be able to pass the SAP BTP Solution Architect certification (P_BTPA_2408). While the post is especially helpful for those aiming to get certified, it also provides useful insights for SAP developers, architects, project managers, and anyone daily involved in SAP BTP projects or operations.

Being a successful SAP Solution Architect or Enterprise Architect requires a deep understanding of both on-premises and hyperscaler technologies, combined with hands-on experience from real customer projects. On top of that, strong expertise in SAP solutions is essential. Earning the SAP Solution Architect certification is just the beginning of this journey.

In these blog series, I’ll share practical insights to help you prepare and succeed to achieve this goal.

🎓 Why Get SAP Certified? Real-World Benefits:

  • Being certified earns immediate credibility customers, partners, and hiring managers are more likely to trust your knowledge and decisions.
  • SAP certification improves your chances of landing your desired role.
  • Certified professionals often qualify for higher rates, promotions, and leadership roles in SAP projects.
  • In some cases (e.g., security reviews, migration, SOC documentation), certification adds weight to your approval or sign-off.
  • The annual certification renewal process ensures your knowledge stays current, especially with cloud solutions and SAP's evolving product roadmap.
  • Certification validates your hands-on skills and prepares you to deliver better results in real SAP implementations.
  • Certified professionals are more likely to be chosen for challenging or high-impact projects.
  • Certification gives you recognition within the broader SAP ecosystem.

Exam Topics and Focus Areas

To pass the certification you’ll need a score of 68% or higher, SAP outlines the following key areas:

  • Understanding the Intelligent Enterprise and SAP’s reference architecture
  • Familiarity with SAP BTP services, account models, capabilities (including AI), suite qualities, extensibility types, clean core, and migration strategies (greenfield, brownfield)
  • SAP Methodologies: Integration Solution Advisory Methodology (ISA-M), Application Extension Methodology, and Data & Analytics Methodology
  • Identity and access management types, CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model), RAP (ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model), and runtime environments
  • DevOps principles, SAP BTP commercial models, and master data replication
  • Recommending solution designs based on requirements and compliance

Key Areas of Study

To gain the knowledge required, focus on the following areas:

  • Enterprise Architect vs. Solution Architect roles
  • SAP BTP Framework and Reference Architectures
  • SAP Discovery Centre, Business Accelerator Hub, and Trust Centre
  • SAP BTP account model (global accounts, directories, subaccounts)
  • BTP Identity and Access Management (IAM)
  • BTP Connectivity and SAP Cloud Connector
  • S/4HANA implementation types: Greenfield, Brownfield, Bluefield
  • Application development frameworks and extensibility
  • Clean Core principles and extensibility models
  • SAP BTP Data and Analytics (SAP Datasphere, SAP Analytics Cloud)
  • SAP Business Data Cloud
  • SAP Data and Analytics advisory Methodology
  • SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology (ISA-M)
  • SAP Integration solution Capabilities and Services
  • SAP Build Process Automation, and Signavio
  • SAP AI tools like Joule, and Generative AI Hub
  • SAP BTP Commercial Models and BTP estimating costs

Deep Dive: ISA-M in Practice

ISA-M is central to BTP solution architecture and used throughout the integration lifecycle:

  • Phase 1: Assess your integration strategy (e.g., cloud-to-cloud, on-premise-to-cloud)
  • Phase 2: Design the hybrid integration platform by mapping use case patterns to technology
  • Phase 3: Define best practices, blueprints, and guidelines
  • Phase 4: Establish organizational and operational models for enterprise integration

If you study the Learning journey (FREE) courses listed below, you'll gain the core knowledge needed for this exam.
For deeper insight into areas you may not work with daily, make sure to check out the advanced learning links shared below as well.
The exam time is sufficient , you will not face any challenges there , and by practicing with the BTP trial environment or your organization’s non-production BTP landscape, you’ll be well prepared to pass the exam surely.

Recommended (Free) Learning Courses

  • Becoming an SAP BTP Solution Architect (main course)
  • Discovering SAP Business Technology Platform
  • Delta Content for BTP Solution Architect

Important Reference Links

  • Integration Architecture Guide (ISA-M)
    NoteBookLM Audio Dialog
  • Integration Solution Advisory Methodology PDF Guide
    NoteBookLM Audio Dialog
  • SAP BTP Guideline Framework
  • SAP Application Extension Methodology
  • ISA-M Presentation (PDF)
  • Extension Architecture Guide
  • SAP Data & Analytics Advisory Methodology Overview

Advanced Learning and Practice

  • Administering SAP BTP (Learning)
  • Create a BTP Trial Account (Tutorial)
  • SAP BTP Admin Guide
  • SAP Trust Centre (Security and Compliance)
  • Connecting BTP and On-Pre using the Cloud Connector (Learning)
  • BTP Account Model & Entitlements
  • OpenSAP : ISA-M Integration Assessment (Video)
  • Integration Suite Assessment Tool (Video)
  • Migration Assessment App (Tutorial)
  • Setting up SAP Mobile Start with SAP S/4HANA on SAP Build Work Zone (Learning)
  • Clean Core Three-Tier Extensibility(Learning)
  • SAP Discovery Centre:  Reference Architectures & Estimators
  • Cost Estimator
  • Cloud Integration Automation Service User Guide

I've put together 15 sample questions to help you prepare for the exam. These questions are a bit more challenging than the ones you'll typically see on the actual test.

1. Which statement is true about SAP trail account in BTP?
a. A BTP trial account includes only 6 GB of memory for applications
b. You are able to move your Proof of Concept from BTP trail to Pay-As-You-Go account
c. A trial account will be removed when a user not logged on after 90 days
d. You need to manually restart of your solution like HANA DB every day

2. Which SAP licensing model In SAP BTP supports: "The total monthly cost would be deducted from the cloud credits balance monthly"?
a. SAP BTPEA
b. Pay-As-You-Go for SAP BTP
c. Subscription
d. SAP Full User Equivalent (FUE)

3. In which level of BTP account you can add or modify your Identity Authentication Service was assigned?
a. Global account
b. Subaccount
c. Directory
d. All answers above

4. In SAP Road Map, SAP decided to have the traditional Early Watch Report in which solutions or platform?
a. SAP Solution Manager
b. SAP Cloud ALM
c. SAP Early Watch alert Workspace
d. DVM in SAP For Me launchpad

5. According to the SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology, the Enterprise Architect is responsible for which of the following activities?
a. Definition of integration standards and technology selection guidelines (cross-vendor, hybrid integration platform)
b. Definition of technical integration architectures with a focus on specific integration technologies
c. Definition and communication of patterns, templates, and best practices
d. All of above
Reference: 
Link   page 51

6. A customer has BOBJ and BODS in their existing SAP on premises landscape, when they are going to migrate to S/4 HANA private edition which solution they can pick to support a similar functionality? Make sure the solution required to be aligned with SAP latest future road map.
a. HANA Cloud, Datasphere
b. SAP Analytic Cloud (SAC), SAP S/4 HANA
c. SAP Intelligent Suite, SAP BW cloud
d. SAP Analytic Cloud (SAC), Datasphere

7. Which statement about SAP Clean Core concept is NOT correct?
a. Deploying a new custom code cannot be on core system (by stack).
b. The clean core strategy advocates for separating custom extensions from the core SAP application.
c. Use "Key User Extensibility" functions of S/4HANA for simple extensions.
d. In classic Extensibility: Applicable primarily to on-premises editions, this tier includes traditional customization methods.

8. Your company has S/4 HANA provisioned in National Security Services (NS2) in Azure, for the entry point of external users you as Solution Architect recommending BTP work Zone as an option.
Which answer meets the requirements (as a cost-effective option) for number 1, 2 descriptions in the diagram below:

a. SAP Build Work Zone advanced edition, SAP Connectivity Service
b. SAP Build Work Zone standard edition, SAP Connectivity Service
c. SAP Build Work Zone advanced edition, Cloud Connector
d. SAP Build Work Zone standard edition, Cloud Connector

9. You are acting as a Solution Architect guiding a migration team that is transitioning from an on-premises SAP PI/PO system to the SAP BTP Integration Suite.
Which of the following tools or services should you recommend to the team, in their appropriate sequence, to support this migration effectively?
a.
SAP BTP Migration Assessment
SAP BTP Integration Assessment
SAP Readiness Check
b.
SAP Readiness Check
SAP BTP Migration Assessment
SAP BTP Integration Assessment
c.
SAP Readiness Check
SAP BTP Integration Assessment
SAP BTP Migration Assessment
d.
SAP BTP Integration Assessment
SAP Readiness Check
SAP BTP Migration Assessment

10. Which of the following SAP BTP services are required or typically used by SAP Cloud ALM?
Select all correct answers (3):
a. SAP Alert Notification Service
b. SAP Automation Pilot
c. SAP Cloud Transport Management
d. SAP Build Work Zone
e. SAP Analytic Cloud
f. SAP Conversational AI

11. Which service can provide a rough rate cost for any service you are going to purchase for BTP solution:
a. SAP For Me Legal & cost
b. SAP Cloud estimator
c. SAP BTP Service Catalog
d. SAP BTP Service Estimator

12. A customer is planning to implement Change Request Management (ChaRM) in the cloud after migrating to SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition on a hyperscaler (e.g., Azure).
Which two main tools or technologies can they use to manage change and transport processes in this cloud-based environment?
a. SAP Solution Manager, CTS
b. Cloud Transport Management System (cTMS), SAP Cloud ALM
c. SAP BTP Cloud Foundry, SAP Cloud ALM
d. SAP Solution Manager, Cloud Transport Management System (cTMS)

13. During the Explore phase of an SAP S/4HANA implementation using SAP Activate methodology, a customer aims to validate fit-to-standard processes and ensure alignment with future-state operations.
They also want to establish a monitoring and task management structure early to prepare for efficient project execution and operations.
Which of the following combinations of tools and capabilities are best aligned to support these objectives? (Choose two.)
a. SAP Signavio Process Insights – to compare legacy KPIs against SAP standard benchmarks and identify transformation opportunities
b. SAP Cloud ALM – to import and manage business process models, simulate variants, and execute fit-to-standard workshops
c. SAP Signavio Process Manager – to model target processes and support fit-to-standard workshops using process content from SAP Best Practices
d. SAP Cloud ALM – to orchestrate project tasks, manage requirements, and support test case management from the Explore phase onward
e. SAP Solution Manager – to configure business roles and monitor production incidents during the Explore phase

14. In the context of SAP Event Mesh and SAP BTP, which of the following are required to establish secure communication between publishers and subscribers? (Choose two)
a. XSUAA service for OAuth authentication
b. Connectivity service with destination type = HTTP
c. Application router for handling protocol mapping
d. Event Mesh instance bound to each application with service keys
e. Transport Layer Security (TLS) tunnelling via Cloud Connector

15. What are benefits of using the Cloud Foundry environment in SAP BTP for event-driven extensions? (Choose two)
a. Native ABAP integration without additional runtimes
b. Horizontal scalability for stateless microservices
c. Event handling with built-in webhook configuration
d. Support for event consumers written in various languages (e.g., Node.js, Java)
e. Built-in support for SAP GUI transactions

Answer Keys:
1-Answer: d; 2.Answer: a; 3.Answer: b; 4.Answer: d; 5.Answer: a; 6.Answer: d; 7.Answer: a;
8.Answer: d;9.Answer: b; 10.Answer: a, b, c; 11.Answer: d; 12.Answer: b; 13.Answer: c, d; 14.Answer: a, d;
15.Answer: b, d

To purchase exam attempts if you don’t have SAP Learning Hub license, you can check the link below:            
https://training.sap.com/content/purchase-certification-attempts

Schedule exam for BTP Solution Architect(P_BTPA_2408)

Share with others and Connect with us!

Please leave your comment if you have anything to add!
If you would like to ask questions, please use the community Q&A.
Give us a like and share on social media if you feel it was useful!
You can follow me in People SAP @Amin_Omidy 
Thanks!

entry.title: User Experience Overviews at SAP Sapphire Orlando & Madrid
entry.content:

entry.title: Optimize SAP ECC Licenses and User Classification Independently with Accelerator for SAM4U
entry.content:
Introduction

Many SAP customers find value in the extended reporting capabilities provided by the Accelerator for SAM4U Adoption (available on the SAP Store) - especially for S/4HANA license management and optimization. However, what about customers who are still operating SAP ECC and plan to do so for several more years? These organizations are still subject to SAP audits and need effective tools for managing and optimizing their SAP ECC licenses.

Let’s explore how Accelerator for SAM4U can also support SAP ECC customers in preparing for SAP audits and optimizing their current license usage.

Analyze Current Classification vs. Authorization-Based Classification

There is no direct mapping between SAP ECC named user license metrics and S/4HANA ruleset metrics. Still, it’s crucial to focus on high-cost license types, such as Professional and Limited Professional users, to unlock potential savings.

For example, in one customer case, the SAP ECC license usage looked like this:

  • 52 Professional Users

  • 53 Limited Professional Users

Using the Authorization Analysis feature of SAM4U’s extended reporting and simulation, we can map users' current manual classifications against their actual usage potential - based on their roles and profiles in the SAP ECC system. This gives insight into what kind of system footprint a user is authorized to leave, aligning more closely with S/4HANA’s licensing logic.

Example: Reclassifying Professional Users

 Pic. 1 – Current Classification vs. Authorization-Based Classification

Let’s focus on the 52 Professional Users:

  • Only 101 users truly have professional roles (mapped to the HB Professional User metric in S/4HANA).

  • 126 users currently classified as Professional Users have roles corresponding only to an HC Functional footprint. These users can be considered, roughly, as 53 Limited Professional Users.

Instead of analyzing each user in the HC Functional group in detail (which is time-consuming), the extended reporting for SAM4U dashboard allows you to drill down and extract a list of these 126 users with a single click. This list can then be sent to your SAP system administrator to update their license metric accordingly.

 Pic. 2 – Users to Reclassify from 52 Professional to 53 Limited Professional

Additionally, there are 184 users classified as 52 Professional Users whose assigned roles do not match any classification in the S/4HANA ruleset.

For these, we recommend mapping them to 59 Core Users - a more cost-effective metric in SAP ECC for unclassified users.

 Pic. 3 – Users to Reclassify from 52 Professional to 59 Core

This rough reclassification already opens up a path to significant license cost savings by reducing the number of users under the expensive 52 Professional Users metric.

Identify Missing Upgrades: Core Users Who Should Be Professional

The final step in this license "homework" is to identify users who are underclassified - those listed as 59 Core Users in SAP ECC, but whose roles match HB Professional in the S/4HANA ruleset.

These users may need to be upgraded to 52 Professional Users to ensure compliance and avoid audit risk. Again, this can be done easily through the drill-down functionality in SAM4U’s reporting dashboard.

 Pic. 4 – Users to Reclassify from 59 Core to 52 Professional

Conclusion

The extended reporting and simulation provided by the Accelerator for SAM4U offers powerful, independent insights into your SAP ECC user classifications. By comparing current user classifications with authorization-based projections (using S/4HANA rulesets), you can:

  • Optimize license allocation

  • Identify over- and under-classified users

  • Prepare confidently for SAP audits

  • Uncover significant cost-saving opportunities

You can request free demo access to the Accelerator for SAM4U Adoption tenant from SAP Store to explore these features and validate your own SAP ECC user classification strategy.

entry.title: ​​​Determining Service attributes from the Service organization​ ​
entry.content:
1. Determination of Planning Plant from the Enterprise Service Org. 

Overview: 

When the system is enabled with the Enterprise Organizational Model, the planning plant in the Advanced execution order is mandatory in the Service transactions. It was earlier updated manually. Now this can be defined in the customization of the Service organization definition. This allows the determination planning plant for execution items within service transactions from the Service organization. In contrast, under the Legacy Organizational Model, the planning plant is determined directly from the service organization unit or entered manually. 

 How to set up a planning plant in the Service organization? 

  • Step 1.  Configure the planning plant in SPRO. 

         Navigate the following Path in SPRO 

         SPRO -> Enterprise Structure -> Definition -> Service -> Define Enterprise Service Organization. 

  • Step 2. Create a Service order with enterprise service organization. During the process, the planning plant is automatically determined from the Service organization. 

 2. Determination of SLA profiles from the Service organization 

 Overview. 

When the enterprise organization is enabled, the SLA profile can be defined within the enterprise structure at the service level. These profiles are automatically determined at the Service transaction items once the service organization is updated, following the access sequence configured in the backend. 

 How to setup SLA profiles. 

  • Step 1. Path: SPRO-> Service -> Transactions-> Setting for Service Transactions -> Define Availability and Response Time. 

 

  • Step 2. Maintain the service profile and response profile in services under enterprise structure. 

         Path: SPRO -> Enterprise Structure -> Definition -> Service -> Define Enterprise Service Organization. 

  • Step 3. Maintain the SLA determination procedures and access sequence. 

          Path: SPRO -> Services -> Transactions -> Setting for service requests -> Define SLA determination                  procedures for service transactions. 

 

 

  • Step 4.  Assign the SLA profile determination procedure for the transaction types. 

Path: SPRO -> Service -> Transactions -> Define Transaction types -> Selection the transaction types.  

Under assignment of Business transaction categories, select services order/quotations, assign the SLA profile in customizing header. 

  • Step 5.  Create the service order, once the enterprise org is determined, SLA profiles will get determined according to the access sequence.  

Note: SLA profiles will get determined only when both the profiles are maintained in the source object. 

 3.Determine Main Work center & Main work center plant from the Service team 

Overview: 

Main work center and Main work center plant are mandatory for the Advanced execution items in the Service orders. Already there is a logic to determine the Service team based on the Service organization as the Service org is one of the attributes in the Service team. 

Now the Service team has been enhanced with 2 new attributes: the main work center and the main work centre plant. Once the service team is determined / updated in the Service items, the main work centre, and its plant for advanced execution items in service orders will be determined. 

How to set up Service Team with Main work center and Main work center plant. 

  • Step 1. Enter the main work center and main work center plant in the responsibility definition of the service team. 

  • Step 2. Create the service order. Once the service team is determined in the service order, the main work center and main work center plant will be automatically derived from the responsibility definition. 

 4. Enhance the Service Team determination via Enterprise Organization. 

Overview: 

When the enterprise service organization is enabled, the service team is determined based on the enterprise service organization setup. 

How to set up service team determination. 

  •  Go to Service -> Master data -> Define Service Team Determination Rules  
  •  Select Condition -> Assign Attributes. 

 Select the ENTERPRISESERVICEORGANIZATION as responsibility definition in the attribute. 

  • Attributes defined within the responsibility configuration will guide the system in determining the appropriate service team in a service order, using a combination of those attributes. 

 For more information on creation of teams:   

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/responsibility-management-create-your-teams-2-5/ba-p/13733021 

For information on creation of service teams: 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/enterprise-service-organization-service-team-management/ba-p/14072189 

 5. Enhance Flexibility of Spare Part Plant and Storage Location in Definition and Determination. 

 Overview:  

To increase flexibility in defining service organizations, the fields for service part plant and storage location are now optional in Enterprise Service Organizations. This change allows for a more adaptable sequence when determining the appropriate service part plant and storage location. 

The determination sequence is as follows: 

  1. Service Team 
  2. Service Employee + Country/Region/Sales Org/Distribution Channel 
  3. Enterprise Service Organization 
  4. Country/Sales Org/Distribution Channel 

Since the plant and storage location were mandatory in the enterprise organization definition, the system would not reach step 4. With this update, making the service part plant and storage location optional allows the system to go further in the determination sequence, increasing operational flexibility.

entry.title: Project Kernseife - improving the measurement of Clean Core in ABAP
entry.content:
TL;DR

To improve the handling and measuring of classic ABAP code in ATC with regards to clean core, the possibility of an ATC check based on a custom classification was developed and is now published as an open source project named "Project Kernseife".

 

Challenges in Measuring Clean Core

Working in the area of clean core - especially focusing on the dimension "extensibility" - in projects of different scales, we were more than once asked in the last years on how to measure and govern the clean core principles within SAP S/4HANA systems (focusing on Private Cloud & On-Premises).

A key part to describe the "clean core state" of extensions is from our point of view the creation of a technical debt score that should align with the governance process in your system. That is why SAP's own code quality tool ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC) is very helpful on this. Since the release of "ABAP Cloud" as target for On-Stack extensions, we have now possibibilities with the related ATC Check Variant (ABAP_CLOUD_READINESS) to identify the "distance to Tier 1".

But looking at landscapes with large amount of legacy code (e.g. > 100,000 custom code objects in classic ABAP) or systems where you cannot use sufficiently released APIs for Tier 1, it becomes more difficult to identify the "real" technical debt.

If you run with the default ABAP_CLOUD_READINESS variant into larger systems, you might be overwhelmed by a huge number of findings in ATC - that are all marked as Error. This is logical, if you want to identify the criticality as "what do I need to change to get into ABAP Cloud", but at the same time the important technical debt might be hidden behind a lot of "false positive" findings.

To improve this situation, SAP released the new 3 Tier check (SAP Note 3565942) in February of 2025. This check improves the situation by analyzing "classic" coding with a stronger focus on technical debt, to identify the "right" things. It is based on SAP's released objects (Tier 1, official APIs) and nominated Classic APIs. These Classic APIs belong either to business logic (e.g. BAPI_PO_CREATE1) or a technical framework (e.g. ALV). Finally, there are now also "noAPIs" (objects that must not be used, e.g. RFC_READ_TABLE) nominated by SAP.

Still - we saw a gap in projects to improve the lifes of lead developers: A custom classification. Because SAP may not be able to classify a certain ABAP object as precise as you may be able to do. So SAP is not publishing any information on a given ABAP object and it is shown as accessing an internal object (which leads in ATC to a warning). But in your use case, you are fine with using it. And this is where Kernseife can help you.

 

What is "Kernseife"?

Kernseife consists of two parts:

  1. A custom ATC Check that is based on the new Tier 3 check mentioned above, but uses a custom classification system.
  2. CAP Application to create a custom classification of ABAP objects (and we even use it to analyze results from systems in a simple way).

ATC Check for custom classification

As mentioned, we started to work on "Kernseife" in 2024 as there was no detailed Tier 3 check available then. Therefore, our focus was initially to create a custom ATC check that can work with a custom classification. It is now re-worked and based on the "new" ATC Check (Note 3565942).

This check is basically analyzing your custom code like the Tier 3 check, but when it comes to the evaluation of the impact of a finding, the custom classification comes into play. You can upload the custom classification as a JSON file and in this classification will then used to determine your findings. Let's explain the difference with an example:

In your project, you need to work with the RF framework, because you are implementing EWM in your Private Cloud Environment. Therefore, you need to work with the classes /SCWM/CL_RF*, that are the recommended way to interact with the RF framework. If you look into the Cloudifcation Repostory, you can see that these classes are not released or nominated as "classic API". Still, you want to generally accept the usage of the RF framework for both: Governance of developers (ATC Checks) and measuring your "distance to clean core".

By using your custom classification which contains the classes /SCWM/CL_RF* as "Business Function - Recommended" (BF1), which will lead to only "Information" (Prio 3) findings in the check and will have a lower impact on the "clean core score", you can improve your efficiency.

For details on the custom classification, you can check the classification guide.

Application to manage Classifications

Of course, you could maintain the JSON file of the classification manually based on the given json format. But we created a little app, that is currently in a feature branch, to do that in a more sophisticated way.

In this application, you can initially check the current state for objects as defined by SAP and additionally add objects that are not yet mentioned by SAP.

Sticking with the example from above, you can now edit the "Rating Code" of the /SCWM/CL_RF* classes to "BF1". After you did your changes, you can download the classification JSON file, upload it into your ATC check system and all of the findings for these classes will in future be recorded as "Information" (Prio 3) message.

 

Governance and Measurability

With this custom classification and the usage in ABAP Test Cockpit, we can now combine the governance view and the measurability within ATC.

Governance

By enhancing SAP's default rating, you can control your developers more efficiently. Because as of today, your lead developers that are able to approve exemptions might be overwhelmed by a lot of "false positive" ATC findings. Just imagine every usage of the example classes "/SCWM/CL_RF*" need to be exempted.

In projects - especially looking at the overwhelming errors from the "ABAP_CLOUD_READINESS" ATC check in classic ABAP, you can now control with your custom classification, that recurring "false positive" findings will disappear (or become prio 3 messages only).

This is important, as - being a reviewer - your chances to ask questions on the important findings will improve. Because if for every major developments you need to approve hundreds of exemption requests, you will most probably go into "autopilot" approval mode as you cannot check all of the requests in detail.

Measurability

Our second motivation to create this check is to create a more realistic impact rating to measure what we call the "Kernseife Score" (basically: a technical debt score per object). With every classification (BF1), we consider a score of how severe the impact of using this SAP object is with regards to clean core.

So you can configure now a score - we did use a 0 to 10 scale - to identify how severe is a single finding. Based on the ATC check, we can now provide per object a score that you can use for your reporting. Either to identify the total score per system, per package, per object - or even to deep dive into analyses like "which object has the worst average score per finding?" or "which object is in total view the "uncleanest" object in my system?".

While you can then easily use your analytics tools (e.g. SAP Analytics Cloud) to create the dashboards you need, in our repository you can at least find a CDS-View you can use for extraction (ZKNSF_I_SCORING), that combines the findings with the score. If you want to directly extract e.g. a list aggregated by object, you could directly use another CDS-View (ZKNSF_I_DEVELOPMENT_OBJECTS).

 

What's next?

Kernseife is currently in use (or in prior versions) in several projects. But to be very clear: There is development ongoing, but we do not have a product roadmap available.

But as it is open source, we are open to feature discussions or pull requests - or just use it for your own advantage!
